recent.statement: The EIA weekly petroleum status report will move CL=F by +1.5%.
recent.evidence: The EIA petroleum report is scheduled for 2026-07-22, after the forecast window closed on 2026-07-21, and no signal shows any CL=F move of +1.5%.

recent.statement: The price of WTI Crude Oil will drop by at least 2% within the next 24h
recent.evidence: The latest WTI snapshot shows only -1.17% (with earlier readings of -1.41% and -1.01%), none reaching the required 2% drop.

recent.statement: The price of CL=F will increase by 2% in the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: WTI's latest snapshot shows 82.06 $/bbl at -1.41%, a decline rather than the required 2% increase.

recent.statement: The EIA weekly petroleum status report will move CL=F by at least 1% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The EIA petroleum report is scheduled for 2026-07-22, after the forecast window closed on 2026-07-21, so it could not have moved CL=F within the window.

recent.statement: The price of CL=F oil futures will increase by at least 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: WTI crude closed at 82.06 (-1.41%), showing a decline rather than the required ≥2% increase.

recent.statement: The price of CL=F will increase by at least 1% in the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: No signal shows CL=F rising ≥1%; the closest oil proxy, Brent Crude (S12), was down -0.93%, indicating no such increase occurred.

recent.statement: A magnitude 6.0 earthquake will strike the Pacific coast of Mexico within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The only Mexico signal (S3) shows a magnitude 5.5M quake on 7/19, below the 6.0 threshold, and no signal reports a magnitude 6.0 earthquake on Mexico's Pacific coast within the window.

recent.statement: A magnitude 6.0 earthquake will strike near the Mexico City area within the next 24h.
recent.evidence: No signal shows a magnitude 6.0 earthquake near Mexico City; the only Mexico event (S3) was M5.5 and predates the window, and such a large quake would certainly have been captured.

recent.statement: WTI Crude Oil price will drop by 2% within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The only crude price signal (Brent) shows just -0.87%, far short of a 2% drop, and no signal shows WTI declining by 2%.

recent.statement: WTI Crude Oil price will drop by 1.5% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: WTI moved from ~82.42-82.61 to 82.39 at window close, a decline far under the required 1.5% drop.

recent.statement: A severe thunderstorm warning will be issued for the Midwest within the next 24 hours due to a low-pressure system moving east
recent.evidence: S2 shows a Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued by NWS Des Moines IA, a Midwest location, within the forecast window.

recent.statement: WTI Crude Oil price will drop by at least 2% in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: The only oil-price signals (Brent at -0.22% and -0.09%) show negligible declines and no signal indicates any ~2% oil price drop, so the forecast did not come true.
